title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson from Everett is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson's participation is driven by memories of the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings and the city's resilient response.
She aims to honor trauma teams whose work underscores skill, compassion, and resilience in crisis situations.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit due to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
Mayor Wu's administration has implemented hiring delays and spending freezes to address the deficit before June 30.
Finance officials expect fiscal challenges to persist into FY 2027 with constrained revenue growth and rising costs.

title: Squirrels, spinach doughnuts, and sirens: New England’s best April Fools’ jokes
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston restaurants featured spinach doughnuts as a new menu item for April Fools' Day.
New England pranksters organized a fake squirrel infestation in several public parks.
Local authorities simulated emergency siren tests as an April Fools' joke across towns.

title: Where do the ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ live?
author: Cameron Sperance
bullets: 'Real Housewives of Rhode Island' debuts April 2, 2026, highlighting cities like Cranston and Newport.
Cranston is featured for its mix of architecture and accessible real estate prices, with homes ranging from $300,000 to $1.5 million.
East Greenwich, Newport, and Providence's East Side are aspirational filming locations with typical home values around $776,458 and $903,896 respectively.

title: She’s running Boston for the hospital that saved her daughter’s life
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Leslie Wallus is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital.
Her daughter Eloise survived a brain tumor with care from MGH's pediatric oncology unit.
Wallus draws inspiration from her daughter's strength and the hospital staff's support.

title: She’s running Boston to support people with visual impairments
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Fransisca Miraningtyas from Jakarta is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the visually impaired.
Miraningtyas aims to raise funds for the Massachusetts Association for the Blind and Visually Impaired.
Her participation is inspired by her own eye condition, pterygium, and the financial challenges many face for treatment.

title: Westfield man busted in alleged Red Bull SNAP scam at Chicopee smoke shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mohamed Yahiya, 47, of Westfield, accused of SNAP and EBT fraud at Adham's Smoke Shop in Chicopee.
Undercover investigation revealed Yahiya exchanged Red Bull purchases for cash, offering $1,731.34 worth for $1,046.
Yahiya charged with food stamp trafficking, tobacco violations, and possession with intent to distribute a Class D drug.

title: Free speech rally planned outside BU president’s office amid Pride flag dispute
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A free speech rally is planned outside BU President Melissa Gilliam's office after Pride flags were removed.
The rally is organized by Terrier Courage, which has gathered over 1,800 signatures for a policy change.
The petition urges changes to BU's policy to allow signage in visible personal spaces on campus.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in April
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: New streaming titles on Netflix, HBO Max, Hulu, and other platforms in April 2026 include anticipated TV show returns and films.
HBO Max features 'The White Lotus' finale, fifth season of 'Hacks', and 'Euphoria' season 3 throughout April.
Several Massachusetts-connected projects debut, including 'The Testaments' on Hulu and 'Widow’s Bay' on Apple TV.

title: What to know about the MBTA’s free and discounted commuter rail plans for summer
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA offers free commuter rail travel on Fridays from June to August, excluding Boston Stadium and special event trains.
Monthly commuter rail passes will be 50% off for summer, saving passholders between $321 and $639, depending on route.
Monthly passholders can bring a companion for $1 each way on weekends, except on special event trains to Foxborough.
